text:
DEPARTMENT OF HEMATOPATHOLOGY
BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE — POST-INDUCTION RESTAGING

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen ID:         BM-2024-XXXX (accession assigned at receipt)
Patient Name:        Margaret Okonkwo
Date of Procedure:   [Day +30 post-induction chemotherapy initiation]
Submitting Service:  Inpatient Hematology/Oncology
Attending Physician: [Hematology/Oncology Attending]
Procedure:           Posterior superior iliac spine (PSIS) bone marrow core biopsy and bilateral aspirate; trephine biopsy right PSIS
Clinical Indication: Post-induction restaging in a 48-year-old female with Acute Myeloid Leukemia with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), WHO 2022 classification; FLT3-ITD positive (high allelic ratio, VAF 82%), ELN 2022 adverse risk. Status post 7+3 induction chemotherapy (cytarabine 200 mg/m2/day x 7 days + daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 days 1-3) plus midostaurin 50 mg BID days 8-21. Peripheral blood WBC recovered to 1,800/uL, ANC 1,200/uL at time of procedure. Evaluate for morphologic complete remission per ELN 2022 criteria.
Prior Pertinent Pathology: Diagnostic bone marrow biopsy [Day 0]: Hypercellular marrow (95% cellularity), 62% CD34+ myeloblasts by flow cytometry, AML-MRC confirmed; cytogenetics 46,XX [18/20] with residual del(7q) in 2/20 metaphases; FLT3-ITD positive by PCR; NGS: FLT3-ITD VAF 82%, DNMT3A p.R882H VAF 44%, SRSF2 p.P95H VAF 38%, ASXL1 p.G646Wfs*12 VAF 41%, RUNX1 p.R204Q VAF 35%.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN RECEIVED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen 1: Bone marrow core biopsy, right posterior superior iliac spine (labeled "PSIS core biopsy") — formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) trephine core
Specimen 2: Bone marrow aspirate, bilateral posterior superior iliac spine (labeled "BM aspirate") — submitted as Wright-Giemsa stained smears (5 slides) and clot section; EDTA anticoagulated specimen for flow cytometry
Specimen 3: EDTA bone marrow aspirate, right PSIS — submitted for cytogenetics and molecular studies (PCR, digital PCR)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen 1 (Core Biopsy): Received in formalin, labeled with patient name and medical record number. The specimen consists of a single tan-pink cylindrical trephine core measuring 1.8 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. The core is grossly uniform without visible foci of necrosis or hemorrhage. The specimen is entirely submitted for decalcification and paraffin embedding in a single cassette. Sections cut at 4 microns and stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E), Giemsa, periodic acid-Schiff (PAS), and reticulin (Gordon-Sweet silver impregnation). CD34 and MPO immunohistochemistry ordered.

Specimen 2 (Aspirate Smears and Clot Section): Five bone marrow aspirate smears received, prepared at the bedside, stained with Wright-Giemsa. Particle adequacy: spicule-containing particles present on 3 of 5 slides, adequate for differential count. Clot section: received in formalin, grossly consisting of a small tan-red clot, approximately 0.5 x 0.3 x 0.2 cm, entirely submitted.

Specimen 3 (Cytogenetics/Molecular): 4 mL EDTA-anticoagulated bone marrow aspirate received in good condition, submitted to cytogenetics laboratory for metaphase karyotype and to molecular pathology laboratory for FLT3-ITD PCR quantification, FLT3-ITD digital PCR (ddPCR), and additional studies as clinically indicated.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Bone Marrow Core Biopsy (H&E and Special Stains):
The trephine core is 1.8 cm in length and adequate for evaluation. The overall marrow cellularity is approximately 25% (markedly reduced from the diagnostic biopsy where cellularity was 95%), consistent with expected post-chemotherapy hypoplasia at day 30 following myeloablative 7+3 induction therapy. The residual cellular areas demonstrate orderly trilineage hematopoiesis with a normal maturational hierarchy.

Myeloid series: Myeloid precursors are present in reduced but regenerative numbers. Normal maturation sequence from myeloblast through mature segmented neutrophil is intact. No morphologically suspicious foci or clusters of immature cells are identified. No Auer rods are seen on the aspirate smears or core sections.

Erythroid series: Erythroid precursors (normoblasts) are present in islands appropriate for the degree of cellularity. Maturing orthochromatic and polychromatic normoblasts are noted. Dysplastic features (nuclear irregularity, multinucleation, ringed sideroblasts) are markedly reduced compared to the diagnostic specimen, consistent with improvement in erythroid dysplasia following blast clearance.

Megakaryocytes: Megakaryocytes are present, 2-4 per high-power field in cellular areas. Morphology is largely normalized; scattered hypolobated forms are noted but significantly fewer than at diagnosis. No micromegakaryocytes identified.

Blasts: Careful morphologic enumeration of blasts on the aspirate differential: 400-cell differential count performed on adequately spiculated smears. Myeloblasts: 1.6% of all nucleated cells (absolute count: 6/400 cells). No monoblasts or promonocytes identified. No Auer rods. This is consistent with blast percentage below the threshold for residual leukemic disease (< 5% by morphology per ELN 2022 criteria).

Additionally, no suspicious blast clusters or CD34-positive aggregates are seen on immunohistochemistry (see below).

Fat spaces: Increased adipocyte content reflecting post-chemotherapy aplastic/regenerating pattern.

Stromal changes: Mild edematous stroma noted. No necrosis or fibrosis beyond baseline MF-1 reticulin seen at diagnosis.

Reticulin Stain: Grade MF-0 to MF-1 (focal, fine reticulin fibrils without intersections), improved or stable from diagnostic MF-1. No collagen fibrosis (MF-3) identified.

Special Stains Summary:
- Giemsa: Confirms trilineage hematopoiesis; no blast aggregates.
- PAS: No abnormal PAS-positive blasts identified.
- Iron stain (Prussian blue): Pending — ordered to assess iron stores and evaluate for ringed sideroblasts in post-treatment setting.

Bone Marrow Aspirate (Wright-Giemsa Smears):
Particles present; adequate for differential. 400-cell differential (nucleated cells):
  - Segmented neutrophils: 28%
  - Band forms: 12%
  - Metamyelocytes: 14%
  - Myelocytes: 10%
  - Promyelocytes: 4%
  - Myeloblasts: 1.6%
  - Monocytes: 6%
  - Lymphocytes: 11%
  - Plasma cells: 1%
  - Erythroid precursors (all stages): 13%
  - Eosinophils: 0%
  - Basophils: 0%
M:E ratio: approximately 5:1 (myeloid predominance appropriate for regenerating marrow post-chemotherapy)
No ringed sideroblasts on iron stain of aspirate smears (iron stain of core section pending).
No Auer rods identified.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Panel performed on FFPE core biopsy sections:

1. CD34 (QBEnd/10 clone):
   Result: Rare scattered CD34-positive cells identified, estimated <2% of total marrow nucleated cells. No CD34-positive blast clusters or sheets detected. The distribution of CD34+ cells is scattered and morphologically consistent with normal hematopoietic progenitors (not aberrant blasts). Endothelial cells are appropriately positive (internal control).

2. MPO (Myeloperoxidase, polyclonal):
   Result: Strong, diffuse MPO positivity in maturing myeloid elements consistent with normal myeloid regeneration. No aberrant MPO-negative blast aggregates noted. Positive controls: satisfactory.

3. CD117 (c-Kit, 9.7 clone):
   Result: Rare scattered CD117-positive cells present (<1% of cellularity), consistent with residual normal hematopoietic stem/progenitor cells and occasional mast cells. No clusters of CD117-positive immature cells. Normal mast cell distribution.

Additional IHC (pending results to be reported as addendum):
- CD3, CD20, CD138 (routine marrow lymphoid/plasma cell assessment): PENDING
- TdT: PENDING (ordered to exclude residual precursor blasts)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FLOW CYTOMETRY IMMUNOPHENOTYPING
(Performed on EDTA bone marrow aspirate, 8-color panel, viability >85%)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Panel: CD45, CD34, CD117, CD33, CD13, CD7, CD19, CD10, CD20, CD56, CD14, CD64, HLA-DR, CD11b, CD16, TdT, CD3, CD4, CD8, kappa/lambda surface immunoglobulin

Results:
- Total cellularity adequate; 50,000 events acquired.
- CD34+ blast gate: 0.3% of all nucleated cells. Importantly, the CD34+ cells in this gate do NOT demonstrate the leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP) identified at diagnosis. At diagnosis, blasts expressed: CD34+/CD117+/CD33+/CD13+/HLA-DR+/CD123+/partial CD7+ (aberrant). CURRENT: The minor CD34+ population lacks aberrant CD7 co-expression and CD123 overexpression; immunophenotype is consistent with normal regenerating CD34+ progenitors.
- No aberrant blast population identified by LAIP-based gating strategy.
- Myeloid maturation: Normal left shift pattern consistent with regenerating marrow. Normal CD13/CD16 maturation arc on neutrophil/granulocyte gating.
- Monocyte compartment: Normal monocytes identified; no aberrant monocyte population.
- B-cell precursors (hematogones): Small population of CD34+/CD19+/CD10+/TdT+/dim CD45 B-lymphoid precursors identified (~0.8% of all nucleated cells), consistent with regenerating hematogones (normal finding in post-chemotherapy regenerating marrow); these should not be confused with leukemic B-lymphoblasts. Morphology and immunophenotype consistent with normal maturation stages I-III hematogones.
- Lymphocytes: Normal CD3+ T-cells and CD19+ B-cells. No immunophenotypically aberrant lymphoid population.
- NK cells: Present in normal proportions.
- Plasma cells: <0.5%, polyclonal by kappa/lambda.

MRD ASSESSMENT BY 8-COLOR FLOW CYTOMETRY:
Using the leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP) established at diagnosis (CD34+/CD117+/CD33+/HLA-DR+/CD123 bright/CD7 partial), the post-treatment analysis demonstrates:
NO LEUKEMIA-ASSOCIATED IMMUNOPHENOTYPE DETECTED.
Sensitivity threshold: 0.01% (1 in 10,000 nucleated marrow cells).
MRD STATUS: NEGATIVE
Conclusion: The results are below the limit of detection using the LAIP-based MRD detection strategy.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CYTOGENETICS
(Performed on EDTA bone marrow aspirate, stimulated culture, G-banding)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Cells analyzed: 20 metaphases
Karyotype: 46,XX [20/20]
Interpretation: Normal female karyotype in all 20 metaphases analyzed. The del(7q) clone identified in 2/20 metaphases at diagnosis is NO LONGER DETECTED. This is consistent with clearance of the MDS-related del(7q) subclone following induction chemotherapy.

Note: FISH panel for del(7q) confirmatory probe (7q22/7q36) and FLT3 locus FISH: PENDING. Results to be reported in addendum.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MOLECULAR PATHOLOGY
(PCR and Digital PCR on bone marrow aspirate)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. FLT3-ITD Allelic Ratio by Standard PCR Fragment Analysis:
   Result: NOT DETECTED. No FLT3-ITD fragment detected above assay sensitivity. Wild-type FLT3 allele only.
   Sensitivity: ~1% (standard PCR capillary electrophoresis).

2. FLT3-ITD by Digital Droplet PCR (ddPCR) — HIGH SENSITIVITY MRD ASSAY:
   Assay: Patient-specific FLT3-ITD assay designed against the 39-bp insertion sequence identified at diagnosis.
   Result: NOT DETECTED. Copies per mL: below limit of detection.
   Sensitivity: <0.1% (1 in 1,000 cells).
   Interpretation: FLT3-ITD MRD is undetectable at high sensitivity.
   Note: Ultra-deep sequencing MRD assay targeting FLT3-ITD/DNMT3A/ASXL1/RUNX1/SRSF2: PENDING. Results expected within 7-10 business days; will be reported as addendum.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ADDENDA (RESULTS PENDING AT TIME OF FINAL REPORT SIGN-OUT)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The following ancillary studies were ordered and results are pending at the time of this report. Results will be communicated as a formal addendum upon receipt:

1. Iron stain (Prussian blue), core section — pending
2. CD3 / CD20 / CD138 IHC — pending
3. TdT IHC — pending
4. FISH: del(7q) dual-color probe (7q22/7q36 loci) — pending (expected 5-7 business days)
5. NGS MRD panel (500-gene targeted ultra-deep sequencing, minimum 10,000x coverage): FLT3-ITD, DNMT3A p.R882H, ASXL1 p.G646Wfs*12, SRSF2 p.P95H, RUNX1 p.R204Q — PENDING (expected 7-10 business days). This assay provides sensitivity to approximately 0.01-0.1% VAF and will serve as the definitive molecular MRD assessment.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY AND PATHOLOGIC INTERPRETATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL CONTEXT: Acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), WHO 2022. FLT3-ITD positive, high allelic ratio. ELN 2022 adverse-risk. Status post 7+3 induction (cytarabine/daunorubicin) + midostaurin. Day +30 post-induction restaging.

PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:

BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE, POSTERIOR SUPERIOR ILIAC SPINE:

MORPHOLOGIC COMPLETE REMISSION (CR) CONFIRMED PER ELN 2022 CRITERIA:
- Marrow cellularity: Hypocellular (25%), consistent with post-chemotherapy aplasia/regeneration
- Myeloblasts: 1.6% by 400-cell morphologic differential (below 5% threshold)
- Auer rods: Not identified
- Trilineage hematopoiesis: Present, regenerative
- Dysplastic features: Markedly diminished compared to diagnostic specimen
- Extramedullary disease: Not applicable to this specimen

MRD STATUS:
- Flow cytometry MRD (8-color LAIP-based): NEGATIVE (sensitivity 0.01%)
- FLT3-ITD by standard PCR: NOT DETECTED
- FLT3-ITD by ddPCR: NOT DETECTED (sensitivity <0.1%)
- Cytogenetics: 46,XX [20/20]; del(7q) clone NOT DETECTED
- NGS MRD ultra-deep sequencing panel: PENDING (addendum to follow)

OVERALL IMPRESSION:
The morphologic, immunophenotypic, and cytogenetic findings are consistent with MORPHOLOGIC COMPLETE REMISSION (CR) WITH MRD NEGATIVITY by flow cytometry and available molecular studies. No residual leukemia-associated immunophenotype is detected. The del(7q) MDS-related subclone is not identified by conventional cytogenetics. Definitive molecular MRD assessment by NGS ultra-deep sequencing is pending and will be reported as addendum; however, the current findings are consistent with deep morphologic and immunophenotypic remission following induction therapy.

Note: Hematogones identified by flow cytometry are benign B-lymphoid precursors consistent with marrow regeneration; these represent a normal regenerating finding and should not be interpreted as residual leukemic blasts.

Note: All pending ancillary study results (iron stain, TdT IHC, FISH del(7q), NGS MRD panel) will be communicated as a formal addendum upon finalization.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGN-OUT INFORMATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Interpreting Hematopathologist: [Hematopathologist, MD/DO]
Resident/Fellow: [Fellow in Hematopathology]
Date of Final Report: [Day +30 post-induction / Day of procedure]
Addendum expected: 7-10 business days (pending molecular studies)
Report electronically signed.

Copies to: Hematology/Oncology Attending; Bone Marrow Transplant Service (cc'd for transplant planning)

CAUTION: This report is based on morphologic, immunophenotypic, and currently available molecular data. Correlation with clinical findings and complete molecular MRD panel results (pending, addendum) is recommended for final treatment decisions, particularly regarding allogeneic stem cell transplant planning in the setting of adverse-risk AML-MRC.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
END OF REPORT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
